summary: Morgan Stanley has fully deployed 'AI @ Morgan Stanley Debrief,' a generative AI tool automating administrative and compliance tasks, across all 15,000 financial advisors. The deployment coincides with a record Q1 2026 in which the Wealth Management division gathered $118.4 billion in net new assets, pushing total client assets above $9 trillion and generating $8.5 billion in segment revenue. The firm's 27.1% return on tangible common equity, seven points above its own 20% target, outpaces Goldman Sachs, which despite a 19% quarterly profit jump remains more exposed to cyclical capital markets without a comparable fee-based asset base. CEO Ted Pick's 'Integrated Firm 2.0' model routes institutional deal flow directly into advisory accounts: investment banking surged 36% year over year to $2.12 billion as the post-2024 M&A backlog unlocked, feeding the wealth engine that now manages assets at nearly twice Goldman's scale.
headline: Morgan Stanley deploys AI Debrief across 15,000 advisors as wealth unit posts record $118B asset surge

key_stat.label: net new assets in Wealth Management in Q1 2026

the_so_what: With 15,000 advisors now on AI-assisted workflows and $118B in quarterly net new assets pushing past $9 trillion, Morgan Stanley is converting AI-augmented advisor productivity directly into asset capture velocity. Goldman Sachs cannot match this without building a comparable fee-based wealth platform, and its capital-markets-heavy model makes that unlikely within this cycle.

capability_evidence.evidence: Morgan Stanley fully deployed 'AI @ Morgan Stanley Debrief,' a generative AI tool automating administrative and compliance tasks for all 15,000 advisors.
